title: cicuta maculata
intro: Cicuta maculata, also known as spotted water hemlock, is a highly toxic and deadly plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. However, due to its high toxicity, it is not recommended for internal use and should only be used topically or in small amounts under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional. The plant can be prepared into herbal preparations such as tinctures, salves, and ointments, which are sometimes used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
